records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the First and Final Departure From Mina
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3017 - In a narration from Ibn Mahbub, from Abu Ja'far al-Ahwal, from Salam ibn al-Mustaneer, from Abu Ja'far (as), he said:
"For the one who avoids indecency (rafath), wrongdoing (fisq), argumentation (jidal), and whatever Allah (swt) has prohibited for him during his state of ihram."
records.arabicText: 3017 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اِبْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ اَلْأَحْوَلِ عَنْ سَلاَّمِ بْنِ اَلْمُسْتَنِيرِ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «لِمَنِ اِتَّقَى اَلرَّفَثَ وَ اَلْفُسُوقَ وَ اَلْجِدَالَ وَ مَا حَرَّمَ اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ فِي إِحْرَامِهِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3017 - In a narration from Ibn Mahbub, from Abu Ja'far al-Ahwal, from Salam ibn al-Mustaneer, from Abu Ja'far (as), he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "For the one who avoids indecency (rafath), wrongdoing (fisq), argumentation (jidal), and whatever Allah (swt) has prohibited for him during his state of ihram."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the First and Final Departure From Mina
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3018 - In a narration from Ali ibn Atiyyah, from his father, from Abu Ja'far (as), he said:
"For the one who fears Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious."
records.arabicText: 3018 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ عَطِيَّةَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لِمَنِ اِتَّقَى اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3018 - In a narration from Ali ibn Atiyyah, from his father, from Abu Ja'far (as), he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "For the one who fears Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the First and Final Departure From Mina
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3019 - It is narrated:
"Indeed, he emerges from his sins as though it is the day his mother gave birth to him."
records.arabicText: 3019 - وَ رُوِيَ : «أَنَّهُ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ ذُنُوبِهِ كَهَيْئَةِ يَوْمَ وَلَدَتْهُ أُمُّهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3019 - It is narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, he emerges from his sins as though it is the day his mother gave birth to him."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the First and Final Departure From Mina
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3020 - It is narrated:
"Whoever fulfills their commitment to Allah (swt), Allah (swt) fulfills His promise to them."
records.arabicText: 3020 - وَ رُوِيَ : «مَنْ وَفَى لِلَّهِ وَفَى اَللَّهُ لَهُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3020 - It is narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever fulfills their commitment to Allah (swt), Allah (swt) fulfills His promise to them."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the First and Final Departure From Mina
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3021 - In a narration from Sulaiman ibn Dawood al-Minqari, from Sufyan ibn Uyaynah, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding the verse of Allah (swt), the Exalted:
"So whoever hastens [to leave] in two days - there is no sin upon him; and whoever delays - there is no sin upon him for one who fears [Allah (swt)]" (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:203):
Imam (as) said: "This means: whoever dies [after completing Hajj], there is no sin upon him. And for whoever's term is delayed, there is no sin upon him as long as they avoid major sins."
records.arabicText: 3021 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ دَاوُدَ اَلْمِنْقَرِيِّ عَنْ سُفْيَانَ بْنِ عُيَيْنَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «فَمَنْ تَعَجَّلَ فِي يَوْمَيْنِ فَلاٰ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ» «يَعْنِي مَنْ مَاتَ فَلاَ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ «وَ مَنْ تَأَخَّرَ» أَجَلُهُ «فَلاٰ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ لِمَنِ اِتَّقىٰ» اَلْكَبَائِرَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3021 - In a narration from Sulaiman ibn Dawood al-Minqari, from Sufyan ibn Uyaynah, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding the verse of Allah (swt), the Exalted:
"So whoever hastens [to leave] in two days - there is no sin upon him; and whoever delays - there is no sin upon him for one who fears [Allah (swt)]" (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:203):
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "This means: whoever dies [after completing Hajj], there is no sin upon him. And for whoever's term is delayed, there is no sin upon him as long as they avoid major sins."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the First and Final Departure From Mina
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3022 - Abu Basir asked Imam Abu Abdullah (as) about a person leaving during the first departure (nafr al-awwal).
Imam (as) replied: "He may leave anytime between now and when the sun begins to turn yellow. However, if he does not depart until the time of sunset approaches, then he should not leave. Instead, he should stay the night in Mina, and when morning comes and the sun has risen, he may then depart whenever he wishes."
records.arabicText: 3022 - وَ سَأَلَهُ أَبُو بَصِيرٍ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَنْفِرُ فِي اَلنَّفْرِ اَلْأَوَّلِ قَالَ لَهُ «أَنْ يَنْفِرَ مَا بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَ أَنْ تَصْفَرَّ اَلشَّمْسُ فَإِنْ هُوَ لَمْ يَنْفِرْ حَتَّى يَكُونَ عِنْدَ غُرُوبِهَا فَلاَ يَنْفِرْ وَ لْيَبِتْ بِمِنًى حَتَّى إِذَا أَصْبَحَ فَطَلَعَتِ اَلشَّمْسُ فَلْيَنْفِرْ مَتَى شَاءَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3022 - Abu Basir asked Imam Abu Abdullah (as) about a person leaving during the first departure (nafr al-awwal).
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He may leave anytime between now and when the sun begins to turn yellow. However, if he does not depart until the time of sunset approaches, then he should not leave. Instead, he should stay the night in Mina, and when morning comes and the sun has risen, he may then depart whenever he wishes."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the First and Final Departure From Mina
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3023 - Al-Halabi narrated that Imam Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about a man departing during the first departure (nafr al-awwal) before the sun declines.
Imam (as) replied: "No, but he may send off his belongings if he wishes, yet he himself should not leave until the sun has declined."
It is also narrated: "Whoever does so is among those who 'hasten in two days.'"
records.arabicText:
3023 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيُّ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَنْفِرُ فِي اَلنَّفْرِ اَلْأَوَّلِ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَزُولَ اَلشَّمْسُ فَقَالَ «لاَ وَ لَكِنْ يُخْرِجُ ثَقَلَهُ إِنْ شَاءَ وَ لاَ يَخْرُجُ هُوَ حَتَّى تَزُولَ اَلشَّمْسُ».
وَ رُوِيَ «أَنَّ مَنْ فَعَلَ ذَلِكَ فَهُوَ مِمَّنْ «تَعَجَّلَ فِي يَوْمَيْنِ» »
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3023 - Al-Halabi narrated that Imam Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about a man departing during the first departure (nafr al-awwal) before the sun declines.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) replied: "No, but he may send off his belongings if he wishes, yet he himself should not leave until the sun has declined."
It is also narrated: "Whoever does so is among those who 'hasten in two days.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the First and Final Departure From Mina
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3024 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Imam Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"It is appropriate for one who 'hastens in two days' to refrain from hunting until the third day has passed."
records.arabicText: 3024 - وَ رَوَى عَنْهُ مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ : «يَنْبَغِي لِمَنْ «تَعَجَّلَ فِي يَوْمَيْنِ» أَنْ يُمْسِكَ عَنِ اَلصَّيْدِ حَتَّى يَنْقَضِيَ اَلْيَوْمُ اَلثَّالِثُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3024 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Imam Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is appropriate for one who 'hastens in two days' to refrain from hunting until the third day has passed."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the First and Final Departure From Mina
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3025 - Jamil ibn Darrāj narrated from Imam Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"There is no harm if a man departs during the first departure (al-Nafr al-Awwal) and then stays in Mecca."
Imam (as) also said, "My father (as) used to say: 'Whoever wishes may throw the pebbles (at the Jamarat) during the height of the day and then depart.'"
The narrator asked: "Until when can the Jamarat be thrown?"
The Imam (as) replied: "From the height of the day until sunset. And whoever hunts (game) has no right to depart during the first departure (al-Nafr al-Awwal)."
records.arabicText: 3025 - وَ رَوَى عَنْهُ جَمِيلُ بْنُ دَرَّاجٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يَنْفِرَ اَلرَّجُلُ فِي اَلنَّفْرِ اَلْأَوَّلِ ثُمَّ يُقِيمَ بِمَكَّةَ » وَ قَالَ «كَانَ أَبِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ «مَنْ شَاءَ رَمَى اَلْجِمَارَ اِرْتِفَاعَ اَلنَّهَارِ ثُمَّ يَنْفِرُ» » قَالَ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ إِلَى مَتَى يَكُونُ رَمْيُ اَلْجِمَارِ فَقَالَ «مِنِ اِرْتِفَاعِ اَلنَّهَارِ إِلَى غُرُوبِ اَلشَّمْسِ وَ مَنْ أَصَابَ اَلصَّيْدَ فَلَيْسَ لَهُ أَنْ يَنْفِرَ فِي اَلنَّفْرِ اَلْأَوَّلِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3025 - Jamil ibn Darrāj narrated from Imam Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"There is no harm if a man departs during the first departure (al-Nafr al-Awwal) and then stays in Mecca."
Imam (as) also said, "My father (as) used to say: 'Whoever wishes may throw the pebbles (at the Jamarat) during the height of the day and then depart.'"
The narrator asked: "Until when can the Jamarat be thrown?"
The Imam (as) replied: "From the height of the day until sunset. And whoever hunts (game) has no right to depart during the first departure (al-Nafr al-Awwal)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the First and Final Departure From Mina
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3026 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the verse of Allah (swt), the Almighty: "Whoever hastens in two days, there is no sin upon him; and whoever delays, there is no sin upon him." (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:203)
Imam (as) replied: "It does not mean that it is merely a choice—whether one chooses to do this or that. Rather, it means that the person returns forgiven, with no sin upon him and no blame remaining for him."
records.arabicText: 3026 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «۝ فَمَنْ تَعَجَّلَ فِي يَوْمَيْنِ فَلاٰ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ وَ مَنْ تَأَخَّرَ فَلاٰ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ ۝» قَالَ «لَيْسَ هُوَ عَلَى أَنَّ ذَلِكَ وَاسِعٌ إِنْ شَاءَ صَنَعَ ذَا وَ إِنْ شَاءَ صَنَعَ ذَا لَكِنَّهُ يَرْجِعُ مَغْفُوراً لَهُ لاَ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ وَ لاَ ذَنْبَ لَهُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3026 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the verse of Allah (swt), the Almighty: "Whoever hastens in two days, there is no sin upon him; and whoever delays, there is no sin upon him." (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:203)
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It does not mean that it is merely a choice—whether one chooses to do this or that. Rather, it means that the person returns forgiven, with no sin upon him and no blame remaining for him."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Staying at Al-Hasba
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3027 - Aban narrated from Abu Maryam, from Abu Abdullah (as), that he was asked about al-Hasba (staying at al-Abtah after completing the rites of Hajj).
Imam (as) replied: "My father (as) would stop briefly at al-Abtah, then enter the houses without sleeping there."
I asked him: "What about someone who hastened in two days—is it required for him to stay at al-Abtah?"
Imam (as) replied: "No."
records.arabicText: 3027 - رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ أَبِي مَرْيَمَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنِ اَلْحَصْبَةِ فَقَالَ «كَانَ أَبِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَنْزِلُ اَلْأَبْطَحَ قَلِيلاً ثُمَّ يَدْخُلُ اَلْبُيُوتَ مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ يَنَامَ بِالْأَبْطَحِ » فَقُلْتُ لَهُ أَ رَأَيْتَ مَنْ «تَعَجَّلَ فِي يَوْمَيْنِ » عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يُحَصِّبَ قَالَ «لاَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3027 - Aban narrated from Abu Maryam, from Abu Abdullah (as), that he was asked about al-Hasba (staying at al-Abtah after completing the rites of Hajj).
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) replied: "My father (as) would stop briefly at al-Abtah, then enter the houses without sleeping there."
I asked him: "What about someone who hastened in two days—is it required for him to stay at al-Abtah?"
Imam (as) replied: "No."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Staying at Al-Hasba
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3028 – Imam (as) said: "My father (as) would stop briefly at al-Hasba and then depart, and it is a place below Khabt and Hirman."
records.arabicText: 3028 - وَ قَالَ : «كَانَ أَبِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَنْزِلُ اَلْحَصْبَةَ قَلِيلاً ثُمَّ يَرْتَحِلُ وَ هُوَ دُونَ خَبْطٍ وَ حِرْمَانٍ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3028 – Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "My father (as) would stop briefly at al-Hasba and then depart, and it is a place below Khabt and Hirman."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Fulfilling the Acts of Grooming (qada' Al-tafath)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3029 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"It is recommended for a man and a woman not to leave Mecca until they purchase dates with a dirham and give them in charity for what they might have done during their state of ihram and for what occurred in the sacred precincts of Allah (swt), the Almighty."
records.arabicText: 3029 - رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «يُسْتَحَبُّ لِلرَّجُلِ وَ اَلْمَرْأَةِ أَنْ لاَ يَخْرُجَا مِنْ مَكَّةَ حَتَّى يَشْتَرِيَا بِدِرْهَمٍ تَمْراً فَيَتَصَدَّقَا بِهِ لِمَا كَانَ مِنْهُمَا فِي إِحْرَامِهِمَا وَ لِمَا كَانَ فِي حَرَمِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3029 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is recommended for a man and a woman not to leave Mecca until they purchase dates with a dirham and give them in charity for what they might have done during their state of ihram and for what occurred in the sacred precincts of Allah (swt), the Almighty."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Fulfilling the Acts of Grooming (qada' Al-tafath)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3030 - Abu Basir narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding the saying of Allah (swt), the Almighty: "Then let them complete their prescribed duties" (Surah Al-Hajj 22:29).
Imam (as) said: "It refers to what happens to a person during the state of ihram. When he enters Mecca, performs the tawaf, and speaks good words, that becomes an expiation for what occurred during his ihram."
records.arabicText: 3030 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «ثُمَّ لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ» قَالَ «مَا يَكُونُ مِنَ اَلرَّجُلِ فِي حَالِ إِحْرَامِهِ فَإِذَا دَخَلَ مَكَّةَ وَ طَافَ وَ تَكَلَّمَ بِكَلاَمٍ طَيِّبٍ كَانَ ذَلِكَ كَفَّارَةً لِذَلِكَ اَلَّذِي كَانَ مِنْهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3030 - Abu Basir narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding the saying of Allah (swt), the Almighty: "Then let them complete their prescribed duties" (Surah Al-Hajj 22:29).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It refers to what happens to a person during the state of ihram. When he enters Mecca, performs the tawaf, and speaks good words, that becomes an expiation for what occurred during his ihram."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Fulfilling the Acts of Grooming (qada' Al-tafath)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3031 - Dharih al-Muharibi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding the saying of Allah (swt), the Almighty: "Then let them complete their prescribed duties" (Surah Al-Hajj 22:29).
Imam (as) said: "The prescribed duties refer to meeting the Imam."
records.arabicText: 3031 - وَ رَوَى ذَرِيحٌ اَلْمُحَارِبِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «ثُمَّ لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ» قَالَ «اَلتَّفَثُ لِقَاءُ اَلْإِمَامِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3031 - Dharih al-Muharibi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding the saying of Allah (swt), the Almighty: "Then let them complete their prescribed duties" (Surah Al-Hajj 22:29).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The prescribed duties refer to meeting the Imam."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Fulfilling the Acts of Grooming (qada' Al-tafath)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3032 - Rabi‘ narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja‘far (as), regarding the saying of Allah (swt), the Almighty: "Then let them complete their prescribed duties" (Surah Al-Hajj 22:29).
Imam (as) said: "It refers to trimming the mustache and cutting the nails."
records.arabicText: 3032 - وَ رَوَى رِبْعِيٌّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي قَوْلِهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «ثُمَّ لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ» قَالَ «قَصُّ اَلشَّارِبِ وَ اَلْأَظْفَارِ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Fulfilling the Acts of Grooming (qada' Al-tafath)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3033 - In a narration by al-Nadr from Abdullah ibn Sinan, from Abu Abdullah (as):
"The tafath refers to shaving (the head) and what pertains to the skin of a person."
records.arabicText: 3033 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلنَّضْرِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «أَنَّ اَلتَّفَثَ هُوَ اَلْحَلْقُ وَ مَا فِي جِلْدِ اَلْإِنْسَانِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3033 - In a narration by al-Nadr from Abdullah ibn Sinan, from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The tafath refers to shaving (the head) and what pertains to the skin of a person."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Fulfilling the Acts of Grooming (qada' Al-tafath)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3034 - In a narration by Zurara from Humran, from Abu Ja'far (as):
"The tafath refers to a man's avoidance of perfumes. Once he has completed his rites, perfume becomes permissible for him."
records.arabicText: 3034 - وَ رَوَى زُرَارَةُ عَنْ حُمْرَانَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «أَنَّ اَلتَّفَثَ حُفُوفُ اَلرَّجُلِ مِنَ اَلطِّيبِ فَإِذَا قَضَى نُسُكَهُ حَلَّ لَهُ اَلطِّيبُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3034 - In a narration by Zurara from Humran, from Abu Ja'far (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The tafath refers to a man's avoidance of perfumes. Once he has completed his rites, perfume becomes permissible for him."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Fulfilling the Acts of Grooming (qada' Al-tafath)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3035 - In a narration by Al-Bazanti from Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as):
"The tafath is the clipping of the nails, the removal of dirt, and the removal of the Ihram (pilgrim's garment) from oneself."
records.arabicText: 3035 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلْبَزَنْطِيِّ عَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «اَلتَّفَثُ تَقْلِيمُ اَلْأَظْفَارِ وَ طَرْحُ اَلْوَسَخِ وَ طَرْحُ اَلْإِحْرَامِ عَنْهُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3035 - In a narration by Al-Bazanti from Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The tafath is the clipping of the nails, the removal of dirt, and the removal of the Ihram (pilgrim's garment) from oneself."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Fulfilling the Acts of Grooming (qada' Al-tafath)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3036 - In a narration from Abdullah ibn Sinan:
"I came to Abu Abdullah (as) and asked, 'May I be your ransom, what is the meaning of Allah’s (swt) saying: "Then let them complete their prescribed duties ('tafath')..."?' (Surah Al-Hajj 22:29)
Imam (as) replied: 'It refers to trimming the mustache, cutting the nails, and similar actions.'
I then said: 'May I be your ransom, but Zarih al-Muharibi narrated to me from you that you said:
"Let them complete their prescribed duties ('tafath')" means meeting the Imam, and "fulfill their vows" refers to fulfilling the rituals.'
Imam (as) said: Zarih has spoken the truth, and so have you. Verily, the Qur'an has an apparent meaning and a hidden meaning. And not everyone can bear what Zarih can bear.'"
records.arabicText: 3036 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ قَالَ : أَتَيْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ جَعَلَنِيَ اَللَّهُ فِدَاكَ مَا مَعْنَى قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «۝ ثُمَّ لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ ۝» قَالَ «أَخْذُ اَلشَّارِبِ وَ قَصُّ اَلْأَظْفَارِ وَ مَا أَشْبَهَ ذَلِكَ» قَالَ قُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ فَإِنَّ ذَرِيحاً اَلْمُحَارِبِيَّ حَدَّثَنِي عَنْكَ أَنَّكَ قُلْتَ « «لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ» لِقَاءُ اَلْإِمَامِ «وَ لْيُوفُوا نُذُورَهُمْ» تِلْكَ اَلْمَنَاسِكُ» قَالَ «صَدَقَ ذَرِيحٌ وَ صَدَقْتَ إِنَّ لِلْقُرْآنِ ظَاهِراً وَ بَاطِناً وَ مَنْ يَحْتَمِلُ مَا يَحْتَمِلُ ذَرِيحٌ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3036 - In a narration from Abdullah ibn Sinan:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I came to Abu Abdullah (as) and asked, 'May I be your ransom, what is the meaning of Allah’s (swt) saying: "Then let them complete their prescribed duties ('tafath')..."?' (Surah Al-Hajj 22:29)
Imam (as) replied: 'It refers to trimming the mustache, cutting the nails, and similar actions.'
I then said: 'May I be your ransom, but Zarih al-Muharibi narrated to me from you that you said:
"Let them complete their prescribed duties ('tafath')" means meeting the Imam, and "fulfill their vows" refers to fulfilling the rituals.'
Imam (as) said: Zarih has spoken the truth, and so have you. Verily, the Qur'an has an apparent meaning and a hidden meaning. And not everyone can bear what Zarih can bear.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Days of Sacrifice
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3037 - In a narration from Ammar ibn Musa al-Sabati:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the days of sacrifice (Adha) in Mina.
Imam (as) replied: 'Four days.'
I then asked him about the days of sacrifice in other towns.
Imam (as) said: 'Three days.'
Imam (as) further stated: 'If a man returns to his family two days after the Adha, he may offer the sacrifice on the third day upon his return.'"
records.arabicText: 3037 - رَوَى عَمَّارُ بْنُ مُوسَى اَلسَّابَاطِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْأَضْحَى بِمِنًى قَالَ «أَرْبَعَةُ أَيَّامٍ» وَ عَنِ اَلْأَضْحَى فِي سَائِرِ اَلْبُلْدَانِ قَالَ «ثَلاَثَةُ أَيَّامٍ» وَ قَالَ «لَوْ أَنَّ رَجُلاً قَدِمَ إِلَى أَهْلِهِ بَعْدَ اَلْأَضْحَى بِيَوْمَيْنِ ضَحَّى اَلْيَوْمَ اَلثَّالِثَ اَلَّذِي يَقْدَمُ فِيهِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3037 - In a narration from Ammar ibn Musa al-Sabati:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the days of sacrifice (Adha) in Mina.
Imam (as) replied: 'Four days.'
I then asked him about the days of sacrifice in other towns.
Imam (as) said: 'Three days.'
Imam (as) further stated: 'If a man returns to his family two days after the Adha, he may offer the sacrifice on the third day upon his return.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Days of Sacrifice
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3038 - In a narration from Kulayb al-Asadi:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the sacrifice (nahr).
Imam (as) said: 'As for Mina, it is for three days. As for other towns, it is only one day.'"
records.arabicText: 3038 - وَ رَوَى كُلَيْبٌ اَلْأَسَدِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلنَّحْرِ فَقَالَ «أَمَّا بِمِنًى فَثَلاَثَةُ أَيَّامٍ وَ أَمَّا فِي اَلْبُلْدَانِ فَيَوْمٌ وَاحِدٌ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3038 - In a narration from Kulayb al-Asadi:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the sacrifice (nahr).
Imam (as) said: 'As for Mina, it is for three days. As for other towns, it is only one day.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Days of Sacrifice
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3039 - Narrated by Saif ibn Amira from Mansur ibn Hazim, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"I heard him say: 'The sacrifice (nahr) in Mina is for three days. Whoever wishes to fast must not fast until the three days have passed. The sacrifice in other towns is only one day, so whoever wishes to fast may begin from the next day.'"
records.arabicText: 3039 - مَا رَوَاهُ سَيْفُ بْنُ عَمِيرَةَ عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ : «اَلنَّحْرُ بِمِنًى ثَلاَثَةُ أَيَّامٍ فَمَنْ أَرَادَ اَلصَّوْمَ لَمْ يَصُمْ حَتَّى تَمْضِيَ اَلثَّلاَثَةُ اَلْأَيَّامِ وَ اَلنَّحْرُ بِالْأَمْصَارِ يَوْمٌ فَمَنْ أَرَادَ أَنْ يَصُومَ صَامَ مِنَ اَلْغَدِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3039 - Narrated by Saif ibn Amira from Mansur ibn Hazim, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I heard him say: 'The sacrifice (nahr) in Mina is for three days. Whoever wishes to fast must not fast until the three days have passed. The sacrifice in other towns is only one day, so whoever wishes to fast may begin from the next day.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Days of Sacrifice
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3040 - It is narrated:
"The Eid al-Adha (sacrifice) lasts for three days, and the first day is the most virtuous."
records.arabicText: 3040 - وَ رُوِي : «أَنَّ اَلْأَضْحَى ثَلاَثَةُ أَيَّامٍ وَ أَفْضَلُهَا أَوَّلُهَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3040 - It is narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Eid al-Adha (sacrifice) lasts for three days, and the first day is the most virtuous."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Greater and Lesser Pilgrimages
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3041 - It is narrated from Mu'awiya ibn Ammar who said:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the Day of the Greater Pilgrimage (Yawm al-Hajj al-Akbar). He said, 'It is the Day of Sacrifice (Yawm al-Nahr), and the Lesser Pilgrimage (al-Asghar) is the Umrah.'"
records.arabicText: 3041 - رُوِيَ عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ يَوْمَ اَلْحَجِّ اَلْأَكْبَرِ فَقَالَ «هُوَ يَوْمُ اَلنَّحْرِ وَ اَلْأَصْغَرُ هُوَ اَلْعُمْرَةُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3041 - It is narrated from Mu'awiya ibn Ammar who said:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the Day of the Greater Pilgrimage (Yawm al-Hajj al-Akbar). He said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'It is the Day of Sacrifice (Yawm al-Nahr), and the Lesser Pilgrimage (al-Asghar) is the Umrah.'"
